finding_results.notes: Arthur's cited lines were shifted from polished moral framing to task-first electrical/household procedure, and an explicit operational limit was added (safe tonight, not tidy till morning; and conditional availability if he is in).

Noreen's confrontation now uses contraction-heavy, colloquial pressure cadence and grounds the argument in immediate house/lane specifics (back-gate bell, front-door routing, wall tapping, who crosses which step).

summary: Both dialogue/idiolect findings were resolved with minimal collateral churn: Arthur now speaks in procedural under-pressure register with explicit practical limits, and Noreen's final exchange is colloquial, contraction-forward, and concretely anchored to physical surveillance dynamics in the house and lane.
